deficits.missing: The from-scratch GRPO trainer uses HF `policy.generate(num_return_sequences=G)` for rollouts and recomputes old_lp with a separate forward pass, but never shows how to align sampler log-probs with trainer log-probs when the policy was set to .eval()/bf16 during generation vs .train() during the loss pass. The toy code silently uses the same weights so ratio starts near 1, but a reader wiring their own loop could get a systematic ratio bias on the first epoch.
deficits.fix: Add one sentence + a two-line assert showing that on epoch 0 (before any opt.step) the per-token ratio should be ~1.0 (exp(new_lp - old_lp) approx 1), and note that dropout must be disabled / dtype must match between the old_lp cache pass and the loss pass.

deficits.missing: The complete SFT loop is single-turn instruction/response and generic; it never demonstrates masking a math chain-of-thought target (the specific deliverable 'SFT for math reasoning'). Multi-turn masking is deferred to 05/02, but there is no worked math-CoT example tying SFT masking to the RLVR reasoning data used two chapters later.
deficits.fix: Add a short worked example that formats a GSM8K-style (question, CoT + \boxed{answer}) pair through the same InstructionDataset._format, showing the reasoning+answer tokens unmasked and the question masked, so the SFT->RLVR bridge is concrete.

summary: Outstanding, reference-quality coverage of every deliverable in this unit. SFT with correct response-only loss masking (05/01) ships a complete runnable PyTorch loop with IGNORE_INDEX, separate prompt/response tokenization to make the mask boundary exact, EOS handling, padding-aware collate, next-token shift, gradient accumulation, LR schedule, and an explicit mask-verification snippet with expected output. The verifiable-reward deliverable (05/09) is exceptional: a real extract_boxed_answer with nested-brace walking and an <answer> fallback, normalize_numeric handling fractions/LaTeX \frac/percent/decimals, math_is_correct with passing assert-based test vectors, a resource-limited sandboxed code runner (rlimits, isolated interpreter, timeouts, hidden tests, graded fraction-of-tests reward), a full rlvr_reward with contingent format bonus and degeneracy guard, and a fully-traced GRPO group worked example. The GRPO/policy-gradient loop (05/08) is the strongest chapter: derivation from REINFORCE + baseline through RLOO to GRPO, a complete self-contained trainer (rollout, z-score advantage with population-std matching the worked numbers, per-token clipped surrogate, k3 KL, multi-epoch), the subtle generate()-EOS-padding mask bug called out and fixed in code, exact tensor shapes throughout, a numeric worked example with correct arithmetic, expected-behavior/failure-signature diagnostics, and the 2025 Dr.GRPO/DAPO fixes (token-level loss, drop-std, clip-higher, dynamic sampling) with a comparison table. PPO/GAE (05/06) and DPO (05/07) round out the optional deliverables with real code (compute_gae, dpo_loss with log-prob masking). I spot-checked the GRPO advantage arithmetic, the population-vs-sample-std note, the k3 KL sign convention (estimates KL(pi_theta||pi_ref) correctly), the PPO clip-branch selection for negative advantage, and the RLVR worked-example numbers -- all correct. No correctness bugs found. A competent reader could build a correct SFT + verifiable-reward + GRPO reasoning pipeline from these chapters alone. Only nitpick-level deficits remain (sampler/trainer log-prob alignment note; a math-CoT SFT worked example).
